# Curator Receipt

## Decision

Kept: this source is institute-worthy because it presents substantial empirical evidence from a large real-world dataset and manual analysis.

## Why

The source clearly meets the admission bar for real empirical substance. It is a research manuscript reporting a large-scale study of 33k agent-authored GitHub pull requests across five coding agents, with quantitative analysis of merge outcomes, CI failures, code-change characteristics, and review dynamics, plus qualitative annotation of 600 failed PRs. This is not commentary or theorycraft; it contains concrete measured results, explicit methodology, and observed failure patterns drawn from real repository activity.

## Limits
